meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master SaaS Pricing & Automated Testing for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Bridge the testing gap in your SaaS projects. Learn to implement robust pricing models and automated testing workflows using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing your revenue strategy and fearing code failures. By integrating automated testing directly into your pricing logic, you ensure financial accuracy and system reliability. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to write robust unit tests, turning your lack of testing experience into a competitive, professional advantage.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, the clock is ticking, and your client expects a production-ready SaaS pricing model by morning. The fear of shipping code that breaks during a payment gateway handshake is paralyzing. You have the vision, but the lack of formal testing knowledge makes every deploy feel like a game of Russian roulette.

It is time to stop fearing the deploy. By leveraging modern AI-assisted workflows, you can treat testing not as a tedious chore, but as an automated guardrail that protects your reputation and your client’s revenue. We are going to transform your development cycle from a 'hope-for-the-best' approach to a rigorous, high-speed engineering operation.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Define your SaaS pricing tiers and logic in a structured format that allows for easy automated verification.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust pricing module schema for a SaaS app. Include fields for tier names, monthly/yearly pricing, feature flags, and trial durations. Ensure the schema supports easy validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a typescript interface for the pricing schema and save it to src/types/pricing.ts'

steps.title: Automating Business Logic Tests
steps.description: Develop tests that ensure your pricing calculations—like proration and tax—never fail under edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for a pricing calculator function. Include test cases for monthly vs yearly billing, discount codes, and edge cases like mid-month upgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file in src/tests/pricing.test.ts using Jest that validates the logic defined in the pricing module.'

steps.title: Simulating Payment Gateways
steps.description: Use mocks to ensure your code handles successful and failed payment responses without needing live API keys during development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock handler for Stripe payment responses. Include scenarios for successful charges, card declines, and webhook timeouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a mock service for Stripe in src/services/stripeMock.ts and integrate it into the test suite.'

steps.title: Implementing CI/CD Guardrails
steps.description: Automate the execution of your test suite so that no code reaches production unless it passes all pricing logic checks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs all tests in the repository on every push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create .github/workflows/test.yml to automate test execution on push.'

steps.title: Refining for Client Delivery
steps.description: Package your automated test suite into a professional report for your client to demonstrate quality assurance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md section titled 'QA and Testing Strategy' that explains how the automated tests ensure the pricing logic is bulletproof.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Append the QA strategy documentation to the project README.md.'

pro_tips: Always test the 'upgrade path'—most bugs occur during mid-cycle subscription changes, not new signups.
Use Claude Code to generate 'negative tests' specifically to see if your code throws the right errors when pricing data is malformed.
Never hardcode currency values; use an integer-based system (cents) to avoid floating-point math errors in your pricing logic.
Automate your test reports to email you a summary after every successful deployment to build trust with your clients.
Keep your test suite fast; if it takes longer than 30 seconds to run, you will stop running it. Use mocks to keep execution lean.
content.next_steps: After verifying your pricing logic, deploy a staging environment using your new CI/CD pipeline to observe live behavior before moving to production.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping with the confidence of a lead engineer. Our community gives you the prompts, the patterns, and the peer support to scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
